rationale.R1.1: Reconstructing the central claim — that panel-based scoring tracks human preferences better than single-judge baselines — is a necessary prerequisite to any substantive check of this artifact.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact draws a direct inference from inter-judge agreement and variance reduction to human-preference alignment; the validity of that inferential chain (internal consensus ≠ human alignment) is a primary load-bearing check target.
rationale.R1.3: The reported metrics (Fleiss' kappa, score-variance reduction, Spearman stability across prompt-orderings) measure internal judge consistency and reproducibility — not correlation with human judgments; whether the evidence actually licenses the human-alignment claim is the most critical check responsibility.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on whether the experimental metrics logically validate the human-preference alignment claim (R1.2 / R1.3 load-bearing), or are you asking for a writing and prose-clarity review (R1.6 load-bearing)?
